documents.label: A copy of the minor's birth certificate
A copy of your birth certificate
A court order
Foster care certification
A copy of your driver's license or other government-issued identification
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
A copy of the minor's Social Security card
A copy of your Social Security card

authority.notes: Listed under 'To show you are the minor's parent or authorized representative' — any one of these is sufficient. The minor's birth certificate appears here (authority) and also under childId; it is the same physical document.

parentId.notes: Listed under 'For proof of your identity' with 'provide one from each category' — the bullets within this category are OR options; any one suffices.

address.notes: The form asks the guardian to fill in their current address directly on the form but does not require a separate proof-of-address document.

childId.notes: The form explicitly states 'please provide BOTH of the following' for the minor's documents, so both are required.

documents.label: Child's birth certificate
Proof of guardianship (if not named on child's birth certificate)
Driver's license or another government issued identification card (e.g. state ID card)
Child's Social Security card
Proof of address (bank statement, utility bill, or insurance statement)

authority.notes: The child's birth certificate establishes parentage/authority when the parent is named on it. If the parent is not named on the birth certificate, a separate proof of guardianship (court order) is required instead. These are effectively OR alternatives for proving authority.

childId.notes: The page lists both the child's birth certificate and the child's Social Security card as required documents. However, the birth certificate also serves the authority category. Both are listed as separate line items in the 'all of the following' list, suggesting both are required for a complete submission. Setting logic to 'and' to reflect that both documents are explicitly listed as required.

mailingAddress.notes: Page lists two addresses: standard mail (PO Box 9554) and overnight mail (701 Experian Parkway). The standard mail address is selected as the primary mailing address. Note: this page covers checking if a minor has a credit report, not explicitly a security freeze — but it is the closest applicable page for minor/child requests at Experian.

documents.label: A document issued by a federal, state, or local government agency showing proof of parentage, including a birth certificate
An order issued by a court of law
Written communication from a county welfare or probation department certifying the protected consumer is in a foster care setting
A guardianship or conservator document
documents.description: A guardianship or conservator document that is current and not expired, includes the protected consumer's name, and that the name of the person on the court document matches the name of the person submitting the request

documents.label: Copy of a driver's license, state-issued identification card, or any other government-issued identification (child)
Copy of a driver's license, state-issued identification card, or any other government-issued identification (parent)
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card (child)
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card (parent)

authority.notes: Page explicitly lists these as alternatives ('such as') for 'sufficient proof of authority'. Any one is sufficient. Note that birth_child (child's birth certificate) also satisfies childId.

parentId.notes: Page groups parent and child identity documents together under step 3 ('confirming your identity AND the identity of the minor'), listing SSN/card, certified birth certificate, and government-issued ID as options. Any one is sufficient for the parent's identity.

address.notes: The protected consumer freeze instructions do not list any address proof requirement. Address proof is only mentioned as optional for standard adult freeze requests.

childId.notes: Page groups parent and child identity documents together under step 3. The same document types (SSN/card, certified birth certificate, government-issued ID) apply to proving the child's identity. Any one is sufficient. birth_child also appears under authority.

mailingAddress.notes: This is the dedicated mailing address for Protected Consumer Freezes, explicitly stated on both the FAQ page and the address-source page. P.O. Box 160 / Woodlyn, PA 19094 is for standard adult freeze requests only and was not selected.
